title: Unique Binary Search Trees II
hints: This problem is related to Catalan numbers.
For a BST with n nodes, if we choose node i as the root, the left subtree will have i-1 nodes and the right subtree will have n-i nodes.
Use dynamic programming: dp[i] represents the number of unique BSTs with i nodes.
Base cases: dp[0] = 1 (empty tree), dp[1] = 1 (single node).
For dp[i], try each node j (from 0 to i-1) as the root. The number of BSTs with j as root is dp[j] * dp[i-j-1].
Sum up all possibilities: dp[i] = sum(dp[j] * dp[i-j-1]) for j from 0 to i-1.
